rules.message: Claude Team requires a minimum of 5 seats. With {{seats}} seat(s), switching each user to Claude Pro ($20/mo) saves ${{savings}}/mo.
rules.explanation: Claude Team at $30/seat has a 5-seat minimum ($150/mo floor). For teams under 5, individual Pro plans at $20/seat are more cost-effective.

rules.message: Cursor Business ($40/mo) for a single user is wasteful. Cursor Pro ($20/mo) offers the same core features. Save $20/mo.
rules.explanation: Cursor Business adds admin dashboard, SSO, and SAML — features irrelevant for solo developers.

rules.message: GitHub Copilot Enterprise ($39/seat) may be overkill for {{seats}} developers. Business ($19/seat) covers most needs. Save ${{savings}}/mo.
rules.explanation: Enterprise features like fine-tuned models and knowledge bases require significant team investment to leverage effectively.

rules.message: ChatGPT Team ($30/seat) for 2 users with moderate usage — consider 2× Plus ($20/seat) to save $20/mo.
rules.explanation: Team features like workspace collaboration add limited value for 2-person teams.

rules.message: You're paying for {{count}} coding assistants ({{tools}}). Most developers only need one. Consolidating could save ${{savings}}/mo.
rules.explanation: Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and Windsurf serve overlapping purposes. Pick the one your team prefers and drop the rest.

rules.message: You're paying for {{count}} AI assistants ({{tools}}). Consider keeping one paid plan and using free tiers for others. Potential savings: ${{savings}}/mo.
rules.explanation: Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini overlap significantly. Keep the primary tool paid and use free tiers for edge cases.

rules.message: Windsurf Teams ($35/mo) for a single user is unnecessary. Windsurf Pro ($15/mo) saves $20/mo.
rules.explanation: Teams plan adds admin dashboard, centralized billing, and SSO — none of which benefit solo developers.

rules.message: Your current AI tool stack looks well-optimized. No immediate savings opportunities found.
rules.explanation: All tools are on appropriate plans for the reported team size and usage.
